reforms.description: The Estonian government has adopted an action plan for national artificial intelligence, aiming to enhance the country's digital capabilities and innovation potential.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing digital capabilities is expected to boost productivity and innovation across various sectors.
reforms.status_evidence: The action plan for national artificial intelligence has been approved.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant strategic initiative to position Estonia as a leader in AI, which is crucial for future economic growth and competitiveness.
reforms.source_quote: In the first hundred days, major deliveries include approving an action plan for national artificial intelligence.

reforms.description: The Estonian government has launched a single nation-wide digital registration system for hospitals, aimed at improving healthcare efficiency and accessibility.
reforms.component_name: Nation-wide Digital Registration System for Hospitals
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving healthcare infrastructure through digital means is expected to enhance service delivery and patient outcomes, contributing to overall productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The single nation-wide digital registration system for hospitals has been launched.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is a major step towards modernizing healthcare delivery in Estonia, with significant implications for efficiency and patient care.
reforms.source_quote: In the first hundred days, major deliveries include launching a single nation-wide digital registration system for 19 hospitals.

reforms.description: The Estonian government is focusing on enhancing access to high-speed broadband and ensuring the stable functioning of digital services to support digitalisation across sectors, particularly outside the ICT industry.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving digital infrastructure is expected to facilitate broader digital adoption, leading to productivity gains across various sectors.
reforms.status_evidence: The government is urged to do more to facilitate a change of pace in digitalisation.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative addresses a critical infrastructure gap that can significantly enhance productivity across multiple sectors, although it is part of a broader strategy rather than a standalone reform.
reforms.source_quote: The government can do more to facilitate a change of pace in digitalisation.

reforms.description: The Estonian government is providing subsidies for last-mile broadband connections, particularly targeting small and medium-sized enterprises, to enhance their access to high-speed internet.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving broadband access is expected to enhance productivity and facilitate the adoption of digital technologies among SMEs.
reforms.status_evidence: Government subsidies for last-mile connections are being provided to small and medium-sized enterprises.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform addresses a critical infrastructure gap that can significantly impact the productivity of SMEs, thus having a meaningful economic relevance.
reforms.source_quote: Government subsidies for last-mile connection, in particular for small and medium-size enterprises are justified.

reforms.description: The Estonian government plans to increase spending on research and development, which is expected to boost innovation across the economy.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased R&D funding is likely to enhance innovation capabilities, leading to higher productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: Planned increases in government spending on research and development are expected to boost innovation economy-wide.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant commitment to enhancing the innovation ecosystem in Estonia, with potential long-term impacts on productivity and competitiveness.
reforms.source_quote: Planned increases in government spending on research and development are expected to boost innovation economy-wide.

reforms.description: Estonia has launched the adult education project Choose IT, aimed at covering parts of the training costs for individuals already in employment, particularly in the IT sector. This initiative is a collaboration between the government and Estonian IT companies, reflecting a successful partnership that enhances workforce skills.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By improving the skills of the workforce, the initiative enhances productivity and supports the digital transformation of the economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The project has already had some success in improving adult education in the IT sector.
reforms.importance_rationale: The initiative represents a significant effort to enhance adult education in a critical sector, contributing to the overall skill development of the workforce.
reforms.source_quote: Estonia pursues a number of policies to cover parts of the training costs for people already in employment. Together with Estonian IT companies, the government has launched the adult education project Choose IT.

reforms.description: The government and manufacturing industry in Estonia have launched a digital skills training project called DigiABC, targeting unskilled workers through their workplaces. This initiative aims to improve digital competencies among the workforce, which is essential for enhancing productivity and adapting to technological changes.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By equipping unskilled workers with digital skills, the project aims to boost productivity and facilitate the adoption of new technologies in the workplace.
reforms.status_evidence: The project has been launched and is actively targeting unskilled workers.
reforms.importance_rationale: The DigiABC project is a significant step towards improving the digital skills of the workforce, which is crucial for the country's economic development.
reforms.source_quote: The government and manufacturing industry has also recently launched a digital skills training project called DigiABC for unskilled workers, targeting the workers through their workplaces.

reforms.description: The Choose IT programme, funded by the European Social Fund and launched in 2017, aims to train 500 postgraduates in software development, resulting in a bachelor degree in programming by the end of 2020.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By equipping individuals with programming skills, the programme enhances the supply of skilled workers in the ICT sector, which can lead to increased productivity and innovation in both ICT and traditional sectors.
reforms.status_evidence: The programme was launched in 2017 with the goal of training postgraduates.
reforms.importance_rationale: The programme represents a significant investment in human capital development, addressing skill shortages in the ICT sector and promoting digital skills among graduates.
reforms.source_quote: The Choose IT programme, funded by the European Social Fund and launched in 2017, aims to let 500 postgraduates attend a six-month software developer programme resulting in a bachelor degree in programming by the end of 2020.

reforms.description: Estonia announced a bill to allow the use of fully autonomous information systems across various sectors, aiming to clarify legal responsibilities and supervision of such systems.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Facilitates the integration of advanced technologies into the economy, potentially enhancing productivity and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The bill is set to be prepared by June 2020.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in regulatory frameworks, enabling the adoption of cutting-edge technologies and positioning Estonia as a leader in digital innovation.
reforms.source_quote: In March 2018, Estonia announced that it will prepare a bill to allow the use of fully autonomous information systems in all areas of life.

reforms.description: The Estonian government is considering implementing a regulatory sandbox for the Fintech industry, allowing firms to test innovative solutions in a controlled environment.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Encourages innovation in the Fintech sector by reducing regulatory barriers, which can lead to increased investment and technological advancement.
reforms.status_evidence: The Ministry of Finance and the Financial Supervisory Authority consider implementing a regulatory sandbox.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative represents a significant step towards fostering innovation in the Fintech sector, although it is still in the planning stages.
reforms.source_quote: The Ministry of Finance and the Financial Supervisory Authority consider implementing a regulatory sandbox for the Fintech industry.

reforms.description: The Estonian Research, Development and Innovation Strategy 2014-20 aims to increase R&D spending to 3% of GDP by 2020, reversing a decline in funding and promoting industry involvement in research.
reforms.package_name: Estonian Research, Development and Innovation Strategy 2014-20
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing R&D funding is expected to enhance productivity and innovation by addressing real-life problems and fostering collaboration between universities and industries.
reforms.status_evidence: The strategy set a clear target for R&D spending and aimed to reverse the decline in funding.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in Estonia's approach to R&D funding, aiming to double spending and enhance the link between research and practical applications.
reforms.source_quote: The Estonian Research, Development and Innovation Strategy 2014-20 set a target for R&D spending of 3% of GDP by 2020.

reforms.description: Innovation vouchers have been in place since 2009 to help SMEs connect with universities and experts for developing innovative solutions.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By facilitating connections between SMEs and research institutions, innovation vouchers enhance the development of new products and services, thereby supporting economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: Innovation vouchers have been utilized to assist SMEs in establishing contacts with universities.
reforms.importance_rationale: The programme provides a significant tool for SMEs to access expertise and support for innovation, contributing to the overall innovation ecosystem.
reforms.source_quote: Innovation vouchers in place since 2009 help SMEs establish their first contact with universities or other experts to develop innovative solutions.
